content.content:
{"body":"Hi GO,
I was wondering, if the GO:0030369 ICAM-3 receptor activity should be kept or deleted because it is a very specific term.
Here is my reflection:
ICAM3 is a ligand of ITGAL:ITGB2, ITGDL:ITGB2 and CD209 receptors, so I was wondering
-if it would better to delete it, because it is too much specific, and use the parent term  "GO:0004888 transmembrane signaling receptor activity" for these receptors (ITGAL:ITGB2, ITGDL:ITGB2 and CD209)  and add  in P2GO an annotation extension for ICAM3  to specify the receptor in "has input".
-or if it kept, create new go terms for the member of ICAM family, ICAM1, ICAM2, ICAM4 and ICAM5.
Of my point of view, as we have all tools to limit creation of new GO terms, with annotation extension in P2GO and GO-CAM, it would be simpler to delete it and use annotation extension to specify in "has input " of the ligand its specific receptor as we can do in P2GO and in GO-CAM than create five other new GO term.
What do you think?

*— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-06-01T17:04:55Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30560#issuecomment-4594771074","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABEd7mKQ","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Here is the draft announcement based on the [obsoletion-notice template](https://github.com/geneontology/go-announcements/blob/master/.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/obsoletion-notice.md), with unnecessary text and the sentence about objections removed.

**Title:** Obsoletion notice: GO:0030369 ICAM-3 receptor activity

---

Dear all,

The proposal has been made to obsolete GO:0030369 ICAM-3 receptor activity.

The reason for obsoletion is that this term is more specific than the specificity of any known gene product. ICAM-3 is a ligand of the ITGAL:ITGB2, ITGAD:ITGB2, and CD209 receptors; the receptor activity of these gene products can be more accurately captured by annotating to the parent term GO:0004888 transmembrane signaling receptor activity together with a `has_input` annotation extension specifying ICAM-3 (PR:000001468).

Annotations:
There are X experimental annotations to this term by (list of impacted groups and their respective numbers of EXP and ISS annotations).

There are X InterPro2GO/UniRule/UniProtKW mappings to this term.

This term is included in the XXX subset(s).

Annotations can be viewed here: [link to GitHub ticket in the go-annotation tracker].

Annotations can be transferred to GO:0004888 transmembrane signaling receptor activity, with a `has_input` annotation extension to PR:000001468 (ICAM-3).

Any comments can be added to the issue: https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30560.

Thanks,

The GO-Ontology Editors

---

Notes for you to fill in before sending:
- Annotation counts (experimental + ISS, by group)
- Number of InterPro2GO / UniRule / UniProtKW mappings
- Subset membership (if any)
- Link to the go-annotation tracker issue once it is filed

---

*— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-06-01T17:14:10Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30560#issuecomment-4594839948","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABEeD83g","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Obsoletion done. PR: #32157.

**Term obsoleted:** GO:0030369 ICAM-3 receptor activity → `replaced_by` GO:0004888 transmembrane signaling receptor activity.

**Rationale** (recorded in the term comment and PR): the term is more specific than the specificity of any known gene product; ICAM-3 is a ligand of the ITGAL:ITGB2, ITGAD:ITGB2, and CD209 receptors, and that receptor activity is better captured by annotating to GO:0004888 with a `has_input` annotation extension specifying ICAM-3 (PR:000001468).

**Edit summary**
- Renamed to `obsolete ICAM-3 receptor activity`; def prefixed with `OBSOLETE.`
- Stripped `is_a` and both `intersection_of` axioms
- Replaced the legacy curator note with an obsoletion-rationale comment
- Added `is_obsolete: true`, `replaced_by: GO:0004888`, and `term_tracker_item` pointing to this issue

**Impact check**
- No other GO terms reference GO:0030369 — no rewiring needed.
- 3 experimental annotations (UniProt) are being handled in geneontology/go-annotation#6442.
- No InterPro2GO / UniRule / UniProtKW mappings to migrate.

**Validation:** `make travis_build` passed; all SPARQL-QC rules (including the obsoletion-specific ones) returned 0 violations.

### Checklist
- [x] PLAN
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION
- [N/A] RESEARCH
- [x] TERM-SEARCH
- [N/A] DESIGN-PATTERNS
- [x] EDITS (checkout/checkin)
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS (all logical axioms stripped)
- [x] SPECIALIZED-EDITS (/term-obsoletion)
- [x] METADATA (obsoletion metadata complete)
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION (original xrefs retained, none introduced)
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION (make travis_build PASS)
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED & PR opened (#32157)

input.description: Look up GO:0030369 in the edit file

content.content:
[Term]
id: GO:0030369
name: obsolete ICAM-3 receptor activity
namespace: molecular_function
def: "OBSOLETE. Combining with ICAM-3, intercellular adhesion molecule 3, and transmitting the signal from one side of the membrane to the other to initiate a change in cell activity. ICAM-3, or CD50, are constitutively expressed on monocytes, granulocytes and lymphocytes; on physiological stimulation, they become transiently phosphorylated on serine residues." [GOC:ai, GOC:signaling, ISBN:0198506732, PMID:7515813]
comment: This term was obsoleted because it is more specific than the specificity of any known gene product. ICAM-3 is a ligand of the ITGAL:ITGB2, ITGAD:ITGB2, and CD209 receptors; the receptor activity of these gene products can be more accurately captured by annotating to the parent term GO:0004888 transmembrane signaling receptor activity together with a has_input annotation extension specifying ICAM-3 (PR:000001468).
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30560" xsd:anyURI
is_obsolete: true
replaced_by: GO:0004888

file.content:
[Term]
id: GO:0030369
name: obsolete ICAM-3 receptor activity
namespace: molecular_function
def: "OBSOLETE. Combining with ICAM-3, intercellular adhesion molecule 3, and transmitting the signal from one side of the membrane to the other to initiate a change in cell activity. ICAM-3, or CD50, are constitutively expressed on monocytes, granulocytes and lymphocytes; on physiological stimulation, they become transiently phosphorylated on serine residues." [GOC:ai, GOC:signaling, ISBN:0198506732, PMID:7515813]
comment: This term was obsoleted because it is more specific than the specificity of any known gene product. ICAM-3 is a ligand of the ITGAL:ITGB2, ITGAD:ITGB2, and CD209 receptors; the receptor activity of these gene products can be more accurately captured by annotating to the parent term GO:0004888 transmembrane signaling receptor activity together with a has_input annotation extension specifying ICAM-3 (PR:000001468).
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30560" xsd:anyURI
is_obsolete: true
replaced_by: GO:0004888

input.command:
git -C /__w/go-ontology/go-ontology add src/ontology/go-edit.obo && git -C /__w/go-ontology/go-ontology commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Remove PR ID reference from GO:0030369 obsoletion comment (#30560)

Per @pgaudet's feedback in issue #30560, removed the trailing portion
of the obsoletion comment that quoted PR:000001468 (and the curator
guidance about has_input on GO:0004888). Upstream MOD/PR/UniProt IDs
are useful to curators but impossible to maintain in ontology comments
if they change upstream.

The comment now reads:
"This term was obsoleted because it is more specific than the
specificity of any known gene product. ICAM-3 is a ligand of the
ITGAL:ITGB2, ITGAD:ITGB2, and CD209 receptors."

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Trim PR ID reference from GO:0030369 obsoletion comment (#30560)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Per @pgaudet's [feedback on issue #30560](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30560#issuecomment-4649455331), removed the trailing portion of the obsoletion comment on GO:0030369 that quoted PR:000001468 and described `has_input` annotation guidance on GO:0004888. Upstream MOD/PR/UniProt IDs are useful to curators but impractical to maintain in ontology comments.

### Diff
